description: Automatically scans GitHub pull request diffs with an LLM to detect security risks and posts non-blocking findings as comments, requesting security team review only for high-risk PRs.

trigger.description: Receives GitHub pull_request webhook events for 'opened' and 'synchronize' actions

steps.name: Respond to Webhook
steps.description: Immediately returns HTTP 200 to GitHub so the webhook is acknowledged within 2 seconds before any heavy processing (LLM calls, GitHub API calls) begins. Placed first so GitHub receives ACK regardless of downstream failures.
steps.error_handling: Best-effort — if this fails, continue processing anyway

steps.name: Acknowledge Webhook Immediately
steps.description: Extracts and sets key PR fields from the webhook payload for use downstream

steps.name: Validate PR Event Fields
steps.description: Validates that the webhook payload contains required fields (action, pr_number, repo, sha) and that the action is 'opened' or 'synchronize'. Outputs a boolean valid flag.
steps.error_handling: On code error, set is_valid_event=false and route to silent exit

steps.name: Check Event Is Valid
steps.description: Routes to fetch PR files if event is valid; otherwise silently exits without posting any comment

steps.name: Fetch PR Changed Files
steps.description: Fetches the list of changed files with patches (diffs) from the GitHub API for the pull request. Response format is set to JSON with splitIntoItems disabled so the full array lands in a single item.

parameters.url: =https://api.github.com/repos/{{ $('Validate PR Event Fields').first().json.repo_full_name }}/pulls/{{ $('Validate PR Event Fields').first().json.pr_number }}/files

output_shape.body: array of file objects with filename, status, additions, deletions, patch fields

steps.error_handling: On HTTP error after retries, route to Slack error alert; do not post GitHub comment

steps.name: Prepare Diff and Metadata for LLM
steps.description: Aggregates file diffs into a compact prompt payload for the LLM. Collects all items from step_4. Truncates individual file patches to 400 lines and total diff to 8000 chars. Warns if file count hits 100 (pagination limit). Retrieves PR metadata from the named Validate PR Event Fields node.
steps.error_handling: On failure, log error internally and route to Slack alert — do not post GitHub comment

steps.name: Analyze Diff With LLM Security Prompt
steps.description: Sends the aggregated diff and PR metadata to the LLM (Claude) with a structured security analysis prompt. Uses raw body mode so 'messages' is a proper JSON array, not a stringified value.

parameters.body:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-3-5-haiku-20241022', max_tokens: 1024, system: 'You are a strict security code reviewer. Analyze the provided pull request diff for security risks. Be precise and conservative — only flag issues you are highly confident (>80%) are genuine security concerns. Do NOT flag normal code patterns. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object matching this exact schema: {"risk_level": "none|low|medium|high", "findings": [{"category": "hardcoded_secret|risky_dependency|auth_authz_change|sql_injection|dangerous_function|other", "severity": "low|medium|high", "file": "filename", "line_hint": "approximate line or diff hunk", "description": "concise one-sentence description", "confidence": "low|medium|high"}], "summary": "one sentence overall summary", "false_positive_risk": "low|medium|high"}. If no findings, return risk_level none with empty findings array.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'PR Title: ' + $json.pr_title + '
Repository: ' + $json.repo_full_name + '
Files changed (' + $json.file_count + '): ' + $json.file_names + '

Diff:
' + $json.diff_text + '

Analyze this PR diff for security risks. Return JSON only.' }] }) }}

output_shape.content: array with text field containing JSON risk report

steps.error_handling: On LLM API failure after retries, route to Slack error alert; do not post GitHub comment

steps.name: Check LLM Response Is Valid
steps.description: Validates that the LLM returned a non-empty text response. Sets llm_response_valid boolean flag. Also passes through the LLM content for use downstream.
steps.error_handling: On failure set llm_response_valid=false and route to error Slack alert

steps.name: Gate LLM Response Valid
steps.description: Routes to parse LLM report if response is valid; routes to Slack error alert otherwise
steps.error_handling: False branch routes to Slack infrastructure error alert

steps.name: Parse LLM Security Report
steps.description: Parses the LLM JSON response into a structured security report. Retrieves PR metadata from the named Prepare Diff node. Validates schema, sets risk level, findings count, and whether to escalate. On parse failure, routes to infrastructure error rather than silently defaulting to risk_level=none.
steps.error_handling: On code failure, set should_comment=false, should_escalate=false, and route to Slack alert

steps.name: Gate Parse Succeeded
steps.description: Routes to normal comment/escalation flow if parse succeeded; routes to Slack infrastructure error if parse_failed is true
steps.error_handling: False branch routes to Slack infrastructure error alert

steps.name: Check If Should Post Comment
steps.description: Routes to post GitHub comment if there are medium/high-confidence findings; routes to escalation check otherwise
steps.error_handling: False branch routes to escalation gate

steps.name: Post Security Findings Comment to GitHub
steps.description: Posts the formatted security findings as a non-blocking pull request comment on GitHub via the issues comments API
steps.error_handling: On failure after retries, send Slack error alert; do not crash workflow

steps.name: Preserve Report Data After Comment Post
steps.description: Re-sets the report fields needed for the escalation gate, since the httpRequest node from step 9 overwrites $json with the GitHub API response. Includes summary and high_severity_count needed by the Slack alert in step_13.

steps.name: Check If Should Escalate to Security Team
steps.description: Routes to request security team review if risk is high with high-severity findings; otherwise routes to final log step. Reads should_escalate from Parse LLM Security Report directly to ensure this field is always available regardless of which branch (comment posted or not) arrived here.
steps.error_handling: False branch routes to final logging step

steps.name: Request Security Team Review on GitHub
steps.description: Requests the security team as reviewers on the GitHub pull request using the request reviewers API endpoint

parameters.url: =https://api.github.com/repos/{{ $('Parse LLM Security Report').first().json.repo_full_name }}/pulls/{{ $('Parse LLM Security Report').first().json.pr_number }}/requested_reviewers

steps.error_handling: On failure after retries, send Slack alert; do not crash workflow

steps.name: Send High Risk Alert to Slack Security Channel
steps.description: Sends a structured Slack alert to the security team channel with PR details, risk level, and a direct link to the pull request. Always reads from Parse LLM Security Report named node to ensure data is available regardless of which path led here.
steps.error_handling: On failure, log internally — do not re-alert

steps.name: Log Scan Completion
steps.description: Records final scan outcome for internal audit log including PR details, risk level, findings count, actions taken, and timestamp

steps.name: Send Infrastructure Error Alert to Slack
steps.description: Sends a Slack alert to the security channel when infrastructure errors occur (GitHub API, LLM failures, or parse failures) so the platform team can investigate without posting confusing comments on the PR

body.text:
=:warning: *PR Security Scanner — Infrastructure Error (Silent Failure)*

The security scanner encountered an error and could not complete analysis. No comment was posted on the PR.

*Workflow:* GitHub PR Security Scanner
*Time:* {{ new Date().toISOString() }}

Please check n8n execution logs for details.

steps.error_handling: Best-effort only — if this fails, log internally and exit

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Send Slack alert to sec-alerts webhook; never post confusing partial comments to GitHub; log execution ID and PR context for debugging

output.destination: GitHub PR comment (non-blocking) + optional security team reviewer request + Slack sec-alerts channel for escalations and errors
output.format: GitHub Markdown comment with findings table; Slack plain-text alert for escalations; internal n8n execution log for all runs
output.description: Security scan results are posted as informational GitHub comments. High-risk PRs trigger security team reviewer requests and Slack alerts. Infrastructure errors fail silently on GitHub (no comment posted) but alert via Slack.

credentials: githubApi — GitHub Personal Access Token or GitHub App token with repo read and PR comment/reviewer write permissions
anthropicApi — Anthropic API key for Claude LLM access
security.pii_handling: PR diffs may contain code with PII or secrets. Diffs are sent to the LLM API and should be considered potentially sensitive. Do not log full diff text in n8n execution logs. LLM responses are ephemeral.
security.data_flow: GitHub webhook → n8n (respondToWebhook 200 ACK immediately) → n8n (parse + validate) → GitHub API (fetch diff) → Anthropic API (analyze) → GitHub API (post comment + request reviewers) → Slack (escalation alert). No data is persisted in n8n beyond execution logs.

cost_estimate: 1 LLM call per PR trigger. Using Claude 3.5 Haiku at ~$0.001/1K input tokens and ~$0.005/1K output tokens. Estimated 6000-9000 input tokens (diff + system prompt) + ~800 output tokens ≈ $0.007–$0.013 per PR scan. At 100 PRs/day: ~$0.70–$1.30/day.

test_cases.name: Synchronize event - new push to existing PR
